info.name: Armada API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Armada multi-agent orchestration system.

Version: 0.1.0

## Setup
1. Set the `baseUrl` variable to your Admiral server (default: `http://localhost:7890`)
2. If API key authentication is enabled, set the `apiKey` variable
3. Use the Status > Health Check request to verify connectivity

item.name: Status
item.description: Health check and system status endpoints.

request.description: Returns health status, uptime, version, and port configuration. Does not require authentication.
Returns aggregate status including captain counts, mission breakdown by status, active voyages with progress, and recent signals.
Initiates a graceful shutdown of the Admiral server. The server will stop after a brief delay.

item.name: Fleets
item.description: Fleet (repository collection) management. A fleet groups related vessels (repositories) together.

request.description: Returns all registered fleets with pagination.
Paginated enumeration of fleets with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body. Query string parameters override body values.
Creates a new fleet and returns it with an assigned ID (flt_ prefix). Auto-saves the ID to the fleetId collection variable.
Returns a single fleet by ID.
Updates an existing fleet. The request body is merged with the existing fleet.
Deletes a fleet. Vessels in the fleet are not deleted; their FleetId is set to null.

item.name: Vessels
item.description: Vessel (git repository) management. A vessel represents a single git repository registered with Armada.

request.description: Returns all registered vessels with pagination. Optionally filter by fleetId.
Paginated enumeration of vessels with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Registers a new vessel (git repository). Auto-saves the ID to the vesselId collection variable.
Returns a single vessel by ID.
Updates an existing vessel.
Deletes a vessel.

item.name: Voyages
item.description: Voyage (mission batch) management. A voyage groups related missions and tracks their collective progress.

request.description: Returns all voyages with pagination. Optionally filter by status.
Paginated enumeration of voyages with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Creates a new voyage with optional missions. Missions are automatically dispatched to the target vessel. Auto-saves the ID to the voyageId collection variable.

response.name: Created
response.body:
{
  "Id": "vyg_abc123",
  "Title": "API Hardening",
  "Description": "Security improvements across the API",
  "Status": "InProgress",
  "CreatedUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z",
  "CompletedUtc": null,
  "LastUpdateUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z"
}

request.description: Returns a voyage and all its associated missions.
Cancels a voyage and all its Pending/Assigned missions. In-progress missions are not affected.
Permanently deletes a voyage and all its associated missions from the database. This cannot be undone.

item.name: Missions
item.description: Mission (atomic work unit) management. A mission is a single task assigned to an AI captain agent.

query.description: Filter: Pending, Assigned, InProgress, Testing, Review, Complete, Failed, Cancelled

request.description: Returns all missions with pagination. Supports filtering by status, vesselId, captainId, and voyageId.
Paginated enumeration of missions with full filtering support via JSON body.
Creates and dispatches a new mission. If a VesselId is provided, the Admiral will assign a captain and set up a worktree. Auto-saves the ID to the missionId collection variable.

response.name: Created
response.body:
{
  "Id": "msn_abc123",
  "VoyageId": null,
  "VesselId": "vsl_abc123",
  "CaptainId": null,
  "Title": "Fix login bug",
  "Description": "The login form does not validate email addresses.",
  "Status": "Pending",
  "Priority": 100,
  "BranchName": null,
  "PrUrl": null,
  "CreatedUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z"
}

request.description: Returns a single mission by ID.
Updates mission fields (title, description, priority). Does not change status -- use the status transition endpoint for that.

Transitions a mission to a new status.

Valid transitions:
- Pending -> Assigned, Cancelled
- Assigned -> InProgress, Cancelled
- InProgress -> Testing, Review, Complete, Failed, Cancelled
- Testing -> Review, InProgress, Complete, Failed
- Review -> Complete, InProgress, Failed
- Complete, Failed, Cancelled are terminal states
Transition from Assigned to InProgress.
Transition from InProgress to Complete.
Cancels a mission by setting its status to Cancelled.
Restarts a failed or cancelled mission by resetting it to Pending. Optionally update title and description before restarting. Body is optional.
Returns the git diff of changes made by a captain in the mission's worktree. Checks for a saved diff first, then falls back to a live worktree diff.
Returns the session log for a mission. Supports pagination via ?lines=N and ?offset=N query parameters.

item.name: Captains
item.description: Captain (AI agent) management. A captain is an AI agent instance that executes missions.

query.description: Filter by state: Idle, Working, Stalled, Stopping

request.description: Returns all captains with pagination. The 'status' filter maps to the captain's State field.
Paginated enumeration of captains with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Registers a new captain (AI agent). Runtime options: ClaudeCode, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, Custom. Auto-saves the ID to the captainId collection variable.
Returns a single captain by ID.
Updates a captain's name or runtime. Operational fields (state, process, mission) are preserved.
Stops a running captain agent. Kills its OS process and recalls it to idle state.
Returns the current session log for a captain, resolved via the .current pointer file. Supports pagination via ?lines=N and ?offset=N.

response.name: Log Available

request.description: Deletes a captain. If the captain is currently working, it is recalled first.

item.name: Signals
item.description: Signal (inter-agent messaging) management. Signals are messages between the admiral and captains.

query.description: Filter: Assignment, Progress, Completion, Error, Heartbeat, Nudge, Mail

request.description: Returns signals with pagination. Supports filtering by signalType, toCaptainId, and unreadOnly.
Paginated enumeration of signals with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Sends a new signal (message). Type options: Assignment, Progress, Completion, Error, Heartbeat, Nudge, Mail. Auto-saves the ID to the signalId collection variable.

response.name: Created
response.body:
{
  "Id": "sig_abc123",
  "FromCaptainId": null,
  "ToCaptainId": "cpt_abc123",
  "Type": "Mail",
  "Payload": "Please check the test results and fix any failures.",
  "Read": false,
  "CreatedUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z"
}

item.name: Events
item.description: System event log. Events are generated automatically by the server when state changes occur.

query.description: Filter by event type (e.g. mission.status_changed, captain.launched)

request.description: Returns system events with pagination. Supports filtering by type, captainId, missionId, vesselId, and voyageId. The 'limit' parameter is an alias for pageSize.
Paginated enumeration of events with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.

item.name: Merge Queue
item.description: Bors-style merge queue with batch testing. Branches are queued, tested in batches, and passing batches are landed into the target branch.

request.description: Returns all entries in the merge queue with pagination.
Paginated enumeration of merge queue entries with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Adds a branch to the merge queue for testing and merging. Auto-saves the ID to the mergeEntryId collection variable.
Returns a single merge queue entry by ID.
Cancels a queued merge entry by ID.
Triggers processing of all queued entries. Creates integration branches, runs tests, and lands passing batches.

item.name: Workflows
item.description: Pre-built request sequences demonstrating common Armada workflows. Run these in order.

request.description: Step 1: Create a fleet to group repositories.
Step 2: Register a git repository as a vessel in the fleet.
Step 3: Register an AI captain agent.
Step 4: Create a voyage with multiple missions targeting the registered vessel.
Step 5: Check the system status to see the voyage and missions in progress.
Step 6: View the voyage detail with all its missions and their statuses.
Step 7: List missions for the voyage to see individual progress.
Step 8: View the event audit trail for all activity related to this voyage.
